vitals.location: Eijsden, Limburg, Netherlands (start/finish: Heerlijkheid Breust per route/practical-info pages; the official regulations name Vroenhof — sources disagree, not yet reconciled)
vitals.field_size: 150 km route capped at 500 riders (2026); no published total-event cap
vitals.start_format: Distance-specific start windows, 08:00-10:00 — not a mass start
vitals.entry_fee: EUR30 (65/90/120 km road, 75 km gravel); EUR35 (150 km); optional EUR2.50 medal
route_options: 150 km road: 2,400+ m gain — Camerig loop, Dalhem, Blegny, Côte de Mortroux and Les Waides in the final 25 km; organizer-rated comparable in difficulty to the elite Volta Limburg Classic/NXT Classic course
120 km road: ~1,900 m gain — Gulpen, Mechelen, Vijlen, Camerig, Sippenaeken, then the Mortroux/Neufchâteau finale
90 km road: 1,400 m gain — finale over Côte de Mortroux and Les Waides
65 km road: ~1,000 m gain — South Limburg hills and plateaus plus an excursion into the Belgian Voerstreek
75 km gravel: 1,000+ m gain, ~65% unpaved — GPX-only, not arrowed; suitable for gravel, MTB or cyclocross bikes

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 67. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 5): confirmed noncompetitive (organizer's own regulations say 'no racing character') and vitals corrected from 149.7km/2,000m to 150km/2,400m+. Under the repo's dimension rubric the corrected figures conflict with the stored distance=4/climbing=4 (150km/2,400m imply one level lower on each); recommend a human re-check distance, climbing, field_depth, prestige and organization against both the corrected vitals and the noncompetitive format. Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule.

course_description.character: Starts and finishes in Eijsden (Heerlijkheid Breust per route/practical-info pages, though the official regulations separately name Vroenhof — not yet reconciled), with five route options ranging from a 65 km South Limburg loop to a 150 km course the organizer rates comparable in difficulty to the elite race. The routes cross South Limburg's hilly plateau country, dip into the Belgian Voerstreek and, on the longer options, Wallonia proper. The signature terrain is Camerig, Côte de Mortroux and Les Waides — not the Cauberg or Keutenberg made famous by the nearby Amstel Gold Race, a separate event with a separate route roughly two weeks later. A 75 km gravel option (about 65% unpaved, GPX-only, unmarked) runs alongside the four road routes for riders on gravel, MTB or cyclocross bikes.
course_description.signature_challenge: Côte de Mortroux and Les Waides, back to back in the final 25 km of the 150 km route — the same finale climbs used on the shorter 90 km option, on a course the organizer says matches the elite race's difficulty.
suffering_zones: Camerig — featured on both the 120 km and 150 km routes as a defining climb
Côte de Mortroux — the closing selection point on the 90 km and 150 km routes
Les Waides — paired with Mortroux in the final kilometres of the longer routes
The Voerstreek/Wallonia sectors — cross-border terrain adding real distance and navigation on the longer options

logistics.lodging_strategy: South Limburg is a developed tourist region with standard accommodation options available.

history.origin_story: The public sportive rides alongside a much older elite race with a tangled naming history: Hel van het Mergelland (founded 1973) became Volta Limburg Classic in 2012, then Volta NXT Classic, then simply NXT Classic for 2026 after its Volta naming sponsorship ended. The organizer, still legally named Stichting Organisatiecomité Hel van het Mergelland, calls 2026 the sportive's 15th edition and 2027 its 16th — arithmetic that implies an inaugural year around 2011, though no source directly confirms that year; founded left null pending a direct source.
notable_moments: 1973: Elite race founded as Hel van het Mergelland
2011 (inferred): Public sportive/toertocht launched alongside the elite race
2012: Elite race renamed Volta Limburg Classic
2026 (15th sportive edition, April 6): elite men raced 196.3 km under UCI rules two days apart from the public ride; sportive rebranded NXT Classic
history.reputation: A well-organized, multi-distance South Limburg touring ride riding real sections of an elite race with over 50 years of history — but explicitly positioned by its own regulations as recreational, not competitive.

biased_opinion.verdict: Real Limburg Hills, Explicitly No Racing
biased_opinion.summary: The NXT Classic Toertocht is a well-organized, multi-distance recreational sportive riding genuine South Limburg terrain — Camerig, Côte de Mortroux, Les Waides — alongside an elite race with over 50 years of history under three different names (Hel van het Mergelland, Volta Limburg Classic, NXT Classic). But the organizer's own regulations are explicit: this is a tourist-oriented performance ride with 'no racing character,' open to all ages and abilities, with distance-specific start windows rather than a mass start. Riders share open roads without traffic priority. Five distance options — 65 to 150 km on the road side, plus a 75 km, roughly 65%-unpaved gravel route — give real range, and the 150 km course is organizer-rated comparable in difficulty to the elite race itself. Just don't come expecting a timed, ranked gran fondo.
strengths: Genuine South Limburg climbing terrain (Camerig, Côte de Mortroux, Les Waides) shared with a 50+ year elite race lineage
Five distance options (65-150 km road, plus a 75 km gravel route) covering a wide range of ability and appetite
Reasonable entry fee (EUR30-35) for a well-organized, long-running event
The 150 km route is organizer-rated as comparably difficult to the elite NXT Classic course itself
weaknesses: Explicitly non-competitive by the organizer's own regulations — no racing character, no ranked results, riders start in windows rather than a mass start
No Cauberg, no Keutenberg — the signature Amstel Gold Race climbs are not on this course despite the shared South Limburg region; don't confuse the two events
No published total-event field cap or attendance figure — only the 150 km route's 500-rider cap is public
Open roads without traffic priority, which is standard for a touring sportive but a real difference from a closed-road competitive gran fondo
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate, well-run South Limburg touring ride with real climbing and genuine race heritage behind it — go in expecting a supported tour with a finish, not a ranked competitive result.

final_verdict.one_liner: South Limburg's hilly touring sportive, riding real elite-race roads — explicitly a tour, not a race.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a well-organized, multi-distance recreational ride through genuine South Limburg climbing terrain with real history. Skip it if you want a timed, ranked competitive gran fondo — the organizer is explicit that this isn't one.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want the Cauberg/Keutenberg climbs and a bigger international field: Amstel Gold Race Toerversie (Valkenburg, later in April, a separate event). If you want the competitive version of these same roads: the elite NXT Classic itself, a UCI-sanctioned race.

Kept two genuinely related videos covering the event's elite-race lineage (Volta Limburg Classic, Hel van het Mergelland).

Remaining videos (Volta Limburg Classic, Hel van het Mergelland 1995) cover the road race lineage this event descends from but contain no course-specific rider commentary usable for intel extraction.

eligibility.notes: IDENTITY/FORMAT CORRECTION (2026-07-24 editorial wave 5): this profile is the NXT Classic Toertocht, the public sportive held alongside the elite NXT Classic road race (formerly Hel van het Mergelland, then Volta Limburg Classic) in Eijsden. 15th sportive edition completed 2026-04-06; 16th scheduled 2027-04-04, registration open. Vitals corrected from an unsourced 149.7km/2,000m to the organizer-confirmed 150km flagship route (2,400+ m gain); prior profile also mislabeled this a 'gravel race' in rider_intel text despite being primarily a road event with one of five route options being gravel. The organizer's own regulations explicitly classify the toertocht as noncompetitive ('no racing character') — see catalog_flags. Verified via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research + curl-verification of nxtclassic.nl (200 OK).

catalog_flags.status_note: fondo_rating.discipline is 'gran_fondo' but the organizer's own regulations explicitly define this as a noncompetitive, tourist-oriented performance ride with 'no racing character' — distance-specific start windows rather than a mass start, no ranked results, no traffic priority. Doesn't cleanly fit the gran_fondo taxonomy or its competitive scoring dimensions (field_depth, prestige, organization assume a competitive race format). Recommend a human call on reclassifying discipline or adding scoring caveats, consistent with how flandrien-ride.json flags similar taxonomy mismatches rather than silently changing the discipline field.
